John's Villa — Housekeeping Reference

Trash Disposal Guide

For housekeeping staff after cleaning sessions
📌 Important: Japan's Trash Separation System
Japan has one of the world's strictest waste separation systems. Trash is divided into specific categories, each collected on different days using designated official bags purchased at local supermarkets. Putting the wrong trash in the wrong bag, using an unofficial bag, or leaving trash at the wrong time or location is a violation of local ordinance and can result in the trash being left uncollected — or worse, a complaint from neighbors. Please follow this guide carefully. The neighbors take this very seriously.
⚠️ All trash must be set out before 8:00 AM on the designated collection day — not the night before, not after collection has passed.
Multiple drop-off spots are listed below. If a spot appears unused (no other bags present), it may have been skipped that day — please try an alternate spot instead.
🔴 Burnable Trash (Combustible / General Waste) Twice a week
Collection Days
Every Monday & Thursday
Deadline
8:00 AM
What Goes Here
Kitchen scraps, food waste, paper towels, tissues, diapers, clothing, small non-recyclable items
Bag Required
Kyoto City official yellow bag — sold at local supermarkets
How to Dispose
Place bag inside the blue mesh net at the drop-off spot
Spot A — Photo ①
Spot A — Photo ②
Spot B — Photo ③
Spot B — Photo ④
Spot D — Photo ⑤
🛍️ Required Bag — Burnable Trash
Use the Kyoto City official yellow bag (sold in packs at supermarkets — look for "燃えるごみ" or "可燃" on the shelf label).
Do not use regular grocery bags — unofficial bags will not be collected.
Where to buy: Super Fresco Gojōōhashi Store
📍 Open in Google Maps
Drop-off Spots
Spot A Alleyway drop-off (Photos ①②)📍 Map
Spot B Front entrance drop-off (Photos ③④)📍 Map
Spot D Canal-side drop-off (Photo ⑤)📍 Map
🔵 Cans, Glass Bottles & Plastic Bottles Weekly
Collection Day
Every Wednesday
Deadline
8:00 AM
What Goes Here
Aluminum cans, steel cans, glass bottles, plastic (PET) bottles — body only
Bag Required
Kyoto City official clear bag with green text
How to Dispose
Spot C: Place inside the silver DUSPON metal box
※ If the box is full, use the blue mesh net beside it
Spot E: Leave in front of the yellow sign post
Important Notes
  • Rinse items before placing in the bag
  • Plastic bottle caps and labels go in the Plastics category — remove them first
  • Do NOT mix cans/bottles with plastics in the same bag
Spot C — Photo ①
Spot C — Photo ②
🛍️ Required Bag — Recyclables
Use the Kyoto City official clear bag with green text (labeled "資源ごみ用" — recyclables).
⚠️ Cans/bottles and plastics must go in separate bags — do not mix them together.
Where to buy: Super Fresco Gojōōhashi Store
📍 Open in Google Maps
Drop-off Spots
Spot C DUSPON metal box drop-off (Photos ①②)📍 Map
Spot E Yellow sign post drop-off📍 Map
🔵 Plastic Packaging (♻ symbol items) Weekly
Collection Day
Every Friday
Deadline
8:00 AM
What Goes Here
Plastic packaging & containers marked with the ♻ (プラ) symbol — e.g. food trays, wrappers, bottle caps & labels, plastic bags
Bag Required
Kyoto City official clear bag with green text
How to Dispose
Spots C & E: Leave in front of the yellow sign post
Important Notes
  • Items must have the ♻ (プラ) recycling symbol on them
  • Items powered by batteries or electricity cannot go here
  • Do not put items out after collection has already passed that day
Spot C — Photo ①
Spot C — Photo ②
🛍️ Required Bag — Recyclables
Use the Kyoto City official clear bag with green text (labeled "資源ごみ用" — recyclables).
⚠️ Keep plastics separate from cans/bottles — use different bags.
Where to buy: Super Fresco Gojōōhashi Store
📍 Open in Google Maps
Drop-off Spots
Spot C Yellow sign post drop-off (Photos ①②)📍 Map
Spot E Yellow sign post drop-off📍 Map
📦 Paper & Cardboard Twice a month
Collection Days
1st & 3rd Wednesday of each month
Deadline
8:00 AM
What Goes Here
Cardboard boxes, newspapers, magazines, paper bags, flattened packaging
How to Dispose
Tie securely with string/twine and leave in front of the yellow sign post
※ No bag needed — bundle with string only
Spot C — Photo ①
Spot C — Photo ②
Drop-off Spots
Spot C Yellow sign post drop-off (Photos ①②)📍 Map
Spot E Yellow sign post drop-off📍 Map
🟣 Spray Cans & Small Metal Items Once a month
Collection Day
1st Wednesday of each month
Deadline
8:00 AM
What Goes Here
Empty aerosol/spray cans, small metal items (pots, hangers, etc.)
Bag Required
Plain clear or white plastic bag (any brand)
⚠ Do NOT use Kyoto City designated bags for this category
How to Dispose
Leave in front of the yellow sign post
Important Notes
  • Spray cans must be completely empty before disposal — press the nozzle until no gas remains
  • Do not puncture cans
Spot C — Photo ①
Spot C — Photo ②
Drop-off Spots
Spot C Yellow sign post drop-off (Photos ①②)📍 Map
Spot E Yellow sign post drop-off📍 Map
